title: AST Disturbance: Fairbanks [AK25081129]
summary: On 08/12/2025 at approximately 1940 hours, Alaska State Troopers responded to assist EMS with a disturbance in the area of college road. Troopers made contact with a male identified as Paul Nickoli. After investigation, Paul was arrested and remanded to FCC for disorderly conduct.

title: Annual summer glacial outburst flood begins in Juneau, expected to peak Wednesday
summary: A resident of the Mendenhall Valley sits by sandbags loaded at an event held on July 26, 2025, by the City and Borough of Juneau and the Central Council of Tlingit and Haida Indian Tribes of Alaska. By 1 p.m. that day, more than 100 cars had checked in at the Dimond Park Field House, according to city officials. Each household was allowed to fill 75 sandbags. The event, one of several held by municipal and Tribe officials, was in anticipation of another glacial outburst flood. (Photo by Corrine Smith/Alaska Beacon) A long-awaited glacial flood...

title: AST Indecent Exposure 1: Fairbanks [AK25081070]
summary: On 08/12/2025, at approximately 1609 hours, Fairbanks AST received a report of a male exposing himself in the area of College Road. Troopers located William Smart in the area and placed him under arrest for Indecent Exposure in the first degree.

title: Kratom faces increasing scrutiny from states and the feds
summary: A variety of kratom and 7-OH products — including capsules, cartridges and wellness shots — are on display at a smoke shop in Buffalo, N.Y. States have led the way in regulating kratom, and now federal officials want to ban 7-OH, a powerful compound found in some kratom products. (Photo by Amanda Hernández/Stateline) For years, state lawmakers have taken the lead on regulating kratom — the controversial herbal supplement used for pain relief, anxiety and opioid withdrawal symptoms. Some states have banned it entirely. Others have passed laws requiring age limits, labeling and lab testing. At least half of the states and the District of Columbia have enacted some form of regulation on kratom or its components — building a patchwork of policies around a product largely unaddressed by the federal government. But that may soon change. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration is pushing to ban 7-hydroxymitragynine, or 7-OH — a powerful compound found in small amounts in kratom and sometimes co...

title: Gage, Montgomery and Thomas file for municipal office
summary: After a week of no filings for City office, former Ketchikan City Council member Janalee Gage filed on Friday, August 8, to run for city council. Gage served from 2015 thru 2024. title: Mendenhall GLOF has started
summary: Suicide Basin has begun draining and the annual glacial lake outburst flood (GLOF) has started. The water level in the lake (see graph on the right) is dropping while the river level downstream is rising. Residents of the 17ft lake level inundation zone are being advised to evacuate. title: Sea Grant Fellowship Spotlight: Alaina Plauché
summary: Alaina Plauché is an Alaska Sea Grant State Fellow working as a fishery analyst with the North Pacific Fishery Management Council in Anchorage. She began her fellowship in September 2024 and will soon wrap up her year.

title: Trump plans to meet Putin in Alaska for Ukraine peace talks
summary: This article was originally published by the Alaska Beacon. James Brooks Alaska Beacon President Donald Trump is planning to meet with Russian President Vladimir Putin next Friday in Alaska to discuss a possible peace deal that could bring an end to the three-year-old Russian invasion of Ukraine. The location, timing and other details of the meeting were not immediately available. title: Tuesday, August 12, 2025
summary: Photo: Kettle River. title: Trump remains vague on details of upcoming Ukraine peace talks in Alaska, anticipated Friday
summary: President Donald Trump announces a "crime emergency" in Washington, D.C., during a White House press conference on Aug. 11, 2025. Standing behind Trump are, from left to right, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, Attorney General Pam Bondi, FBI Director Kash Patel and U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia Jeanine Pirro. (Image via White House livestream) President Donald Trump’s high-profile meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin adjourned their brief summit Friday without announcing a breakthrough in negotiations to end Russia’s three-year-old invasion of Ukraine. “We didn’t get there, but we have a very good chance of getting there,” Trump said af...

title: AST Traffic: North Pole [AK25080723]
summary: On 08/11/2025 at approximately 23:41, Troopers attempted to stop a motorcycle traveling westbound on Holmes Rd. AST pursued the vehicle onto the Richardson Highway reaching speeds of 124 miles per hour. The motorcycle turned onto the Old Richardson Highway and was not able to be located.
